IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 190 metres across 4 sections of the network.
Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, A longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This work is taking place to the rear of Staines Farm near the A2.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 07:45 - Thank you for your patience while our engineers repair a fault on the underground network in the St. Ives  area, we've now restored power to most of the affected properties. There are now only 2 properties without power, who we'll speak to directly. If you're without power and haven't been contacted, then we'll need to hear from you. Please call us on 08003163105 (free from landlines and mobiles).

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the unexpected outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the St. Ives area and are investigating the fault. You may find your power is going on and off; that is normal for this type of issue and is due to our engineers working to divert power remotely. By doing this, we've managed to restore power to 30 customers. There are now 40 properties still affected and using an average time frame we're estimating all power will be on between 09:00-10:00, but this may change later depending on what our staff find. We'll keep sending SMS updates until everybody is restored. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Sent by Decasta at 07:28.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?
This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're working to restore power as quickly as possible, including remotely redirecting electricity, so you may notice the power going on and off. Once on site, engineers will: check the network, try again to restore power, and if needed they will work to locate the issue and make repairs.

We are on our way to the St. Ives  area and believe the issue is with an underground network fault. We estimate you'll be back on between 09:30-10:30 but this may change as we learn more - we'll keep you posted
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work will cover approximately 250 metres across 5 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This is taking place near West End Goudhurst Road. Two way traffic lights will be in place to allow the works to be carried out safely.

Steps.Message:

We have been alerted to a potentially dangerous situation involving an overhead cable on the ground, so have responded as quickly as possible and asked our control room to turn power off to the area using our high voltage network. This is why a wide area has lost electricity.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather.
To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 60 metres across 14 sections of the network.
Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, A longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages as we appreciate you may have been impacted by an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Hasketon Road.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 09:12 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?
This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're working to restore power as quickly as possible, including remotely redirecting electricity, so you may notice the power going on and off. Once on site, engineers will: check the network, try again to restore power, and if needed they will work to locate the issue and make repairs.

We are on our way to the Upminster RM14 area and believe the issue is with an overhead network fault. We estimate you'll be back on between 08:30-09:30 but this may change as we learn more - we'll keep you posted

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: An underground electricity cable faulted on our high voltage network, causing an area wide power cut.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Luke at 01:35 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all 3858 customers in the Sutton, SM1 4 area by diverting power around the underground fault. We're pleased that we have been able to restore power sooner than we estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.

Sent by Luke at 01:03 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground fault that initially interrupted power to 3858 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 296 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 04:00 and 05:00 but this may change depending on what our staff find.

Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.

Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off to upgrade the equipment. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our engineers may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. This work is taking place in and around West Tofts Area.

Once complete this work will adhere to the customer request, ensure our network can uphold the increased electricity usage in your area and it will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place just off London Road.
